name: Large Volume Scenario (LVS) moduli stabilisation
summary: An alternative to KKLT for stabilising Type IIB string compactifications: combines tree-level flux superpotential W_0, leading alpha'-corrections, and non-perturbative contributions to fix the Calabi-Yau volume V at exponentially large values V ~ exp(c/g_s) (typically 10^5 - 10^15 in string units). Naturally produces low-scale supersymmetry breaking with m_3/2 ~ M_pl / V and TeV-scale soft masses for V ~ 10^15. Generates a 'Swiss cheese' Calabi-Yau (large bulk + small blow-up cycles) and predicts characteristic moduli signatures - light axions from large cycles, heavier moduli from blow-up cycles.

addresses_puzzles.note: Provides explicit large family of (semi-)realistic Type IIB string vacua with non-singular moduli stabilisation, controlled corrections, and accessible phenomenology. Helps populate the landscape with concrete examples rather than abstract counting.
TeV-scale supersymmetry naturally emerges from exponentially large volume V ~ M_pl^2 / m_3/2^2; provides string-theoretic UV completion for low-scale SUSY without ad-hoc anthropic tuning.

Like KKLT, requires uplift to de Sitter; LVS uplift mechanisms (D-term, anti-D3-brane on the warped throat, T-brane uplift) face the same swampland-conjecture pressure. de Sitter status remains contested.

new_scales: string scale M_s ~ M_pl / sqrt(V) ~ 10^15 GeV (for TeV SUSY)
introduces.note: Flux superpotential W_0, string coupling g_s, large-volume parameter V (these are derived from microscopic flux quanta, but free in the effective description).

history: Balasubramanian, Berglund, Conlon, Quevedo (2005), 'Systematics of moduli stabilisation in Calabi-Yau flux compactifications', JHEP 03, 007, arXiv:hep-th/0502058 - founding paper. Conlon-Quevedo (2006); large body of LVS phenomenology by Quevedo group, Cicoli et al. 2007-present.

references.citation: Balasubramanian, Berglund, Conlon, Quevedo, 'Systematics of moduli stabilisation in Calabi-Yau flux compactifications', JHEP 03, 007 (2005)
